data.content: Let me search for the Crucible source/

result.result:
1→# ⚗️ Crucible 2→ 3→[![CI](https://github.com/Mootikins/crucible/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/Mootikins/crucible/actions/workflows/ci.yml) 4→[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT%2FApache--2.0-blue.svg)](LICENSE-MIT) 5→[![Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-mootikins.github.io%2Fcrucible-blue)](https://mootikins.github.io/crucible/) 6→ 7→**A knowledge-grounded agent runtime. Agents that draw from a knowledge graph make better decisions.** 8→ 9→Local-first. No cloud. No lock-in. Your conversations, notes, and wikilinks form a knowledge graph that agents draw from and contribute to — all as markdown files you own. 10→ 11→

12→ 13→

14→ 15→> **Early Development**: APIs and storage formats may change. Contributions welcome! 16→ 17→## What Makes Crucible Different 18→ 19→Memory and knowledge are too fundamental to be an afterthought. Most AI tools treat conversations as disposable — Crucible makes them the foundation. 20→ 21→- **Knowledge-grounded agents.** Precognition auto-injects relevant context from your knowledge graph before each LLM turn. Block-level embeddings power semantic search at paragraph granularity. The more you use it, the smarter your agents get. 22→- **Sessions are notes.** Every chat saves as markdown in your kiln. Search them, link them with wikilinks, version them in git. Conversations become permanent, connectable knowledge. 23→- **Neovim-like architecture.** Lua/Fennel plugins, TUI-first, headless daemon with RPC. Most behaviors beyond the knowledge core can be scripted. 24→- **Bring any LLM.** Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, local GGUF models. Swap freely. 25→- **Plaintext first.** No proprietary formats. Files are the source of truth. The database is optional acceleration. 26→ 27→## How It Compares 28→ 29→| | Crucible | ChatGPT | Obsidian + AI | OpenClaw | 30→|---|---|---|---|---| 31→| Local-first | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | 32→| Sessions as markdown | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | 33→| Knowledge graph | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | 34→| Bring your own LLM | ✅ | ❌ | Partial | ✅ | 35→| Plugin system | ✅ Lua/Fennel | ❌ | ✅ JS | ✅ TS | 36→| MCP server | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | 37→| Semantic search | ✅ Block-level | ❌ | Plugin | ❌ | 38→| Setup time | ~2 min | 0 | ~5 min | 2-7 hrs | 39→ 40→## Install 41→ 42→**Pre-built binaries** (Linux x86_64/aarch64, macOS Apple Silicon): 43→ 44→```bash 45→curl -fsSL https://github.com/Mootikins/crucible/releases/latest/download/crucible-cli-installer.sh | sh 46→``` 47→ 48→**From source:** 49→ 50→```bash 51→cargo install --git https://github.com/Mootikins/crucible.git crucible-cli 52→``` 53→ 54→## Quick Start 55→ 56→```bash 57→# Start a chat session 58→cru chat 59→ 60→# Chat with Claude Code, enriched by your knowledge base 61→cru chat -a claude 62→ 63→# Or start the MCP server for Claude/GPT integration 64→cru mcp 65→``` 66→ 67→First run prompts for a kiln path and detects available LLM providers. A background daemon auto-spawns via `cru daemon serve` to manage session state, file watching, and multi-session support. It communicates over a Unix socket and restarts automatically if stopped. 68→ 69→**In a chat session:** 70→- Type naturally, the agent responds with access to your knowledge base 71→- `/search query` injects relevant notes into context 72→- `:model`, `:set`, `:export` for REPL commands 73→- `BackTab` cycles modes: Normal → Plan → Auto 74→ 75→

76→ 77→

78→ 79→## Features 80→ 81→### Agent Chat 82→ 83→Interactive conversations with full session persistence. The TUI supports streaming markdown, tool calls, and multi-turn context. Sessions save as markdown files organized by workspace. 84→ 85→### Knowledge Graph 86→ 87→Wikilinks (`[[Note Name]]`) define your graph. No extraction step, no special syntax beyond what you'd write naturally. Query by graph traversal, semantic similarity, tags, or full-text search. 88→ 89→### MCP Server 90→ 91→Expose your knowledge base to any MCP-compatible AI (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, GPT, local models): 92→ 93→```bash 94→cru mcp 95→``` 96→ 97→Tools include `semantic_search`, `create_note`, `get_outlinks`, `get_inlinks`, and more. 98→ 99→### Agent Integration (ACP) 100→ 101→Crucible can spawn and orchestrate external AI agents through the [Agent Context Protocol](https://agentcontextprotocol.org/). Your agent gets full access to Crucible's knowledge graph, semantic search, and tools. 102→ 103→```bash 104→# Use Claude Code with your knowledge base 105→cru chat -a claude 106→ 107→# Use OpenCode 108→cru chat -a opencode 109→ 110→# Use Gemini CLI 111→cru chat -a gemini 112→``` 113→ 114→Built-in agents (auto-discovered if installed): 115→ 116→| Agent | Command | Install | 117→|-------|---------|---------| 118→| opencode | `opencode` | `go install github.com/grafana/opencode@latest` | 119→| claude | `npx @zed-industries/claude-agent-acp` | `npm install -g @zed-industries/claude-agent-acp` | 120→| gemini | `gemini` | `npm install -g gemini-cli` | 121→| codex | `npx @zed-industries/codex-acp` | `npm install -g @zed-industries/codex-acp` | 122→| cursor | `cursor-acp` | `npm install -g cursor-acp` | 123→ 124→Agents can delegate tasks to each other. An ACP agent like Claude can hand off work to Cursor or OpenCode mid-conversation using the `delegate_session` tool, then incorporate the results. Delegation works both directions: internal agents can delegate to ACP agents, and ACP agents can delegate to other ACP agents. 125→ 126→Custom profiles go in `crucible.toml`: 127→ 128→```toml 129→[acp.agents.my-claude] 130→extends = "claude" 131→env = { ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL = "http://localhost:4000" } 132→``` 133→ 134→Then: `cru chat -a my-claude` 135→ 136→### Lua Plugins 137→ 138→Drop `.lua` or `.fnl` files into `~/.config/crucible/plugins/` or your kiln's `plugins/` directory: 139→ 140→```lua 141→-- @tool name="summarize" description="Summarize notes matching query" 142→-- @param query string "Search query" 143→function summarize(args) 144→ local results = crucible.search(args.query) 145→ return { summary = "Found " .. #results .. " notes" } 146→end 147→``` 148→ 149→See the [plugin guide](./docs/Help/Concepts/Scripting%20Languages.md) for the full API. 150→ 151→## Documentation 152→ 153→- **[Documentation Site](https://mootikins.github.io/crucible/)** — searchable, organized reference 154→- **[docs/](./docs/)** is both the user guide and a working example kiln (155 interlinked notes) 155→- **[AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md)** covers architecture and AI agent instructions 156→ 157→## Command Reference 158→ 159→| Command | Alias | Description | 160→|---------|-------|-------------| 161→| `cru chat` | `c` | Interactive AI chat with session persistence | 162→| `cru chat -a ` | | Use an ACP agent (claude, opencode, gemini, etc.) | 163→| `cru chat --resume ` | | Resume a previous session | 164→| `cru mcp` | | Start MCP server for external AI agents | 165→| `cru process` | `p` | Parse, enrich, and store markdown files | 166→| `cru init` | `i` | Initialize a new kiln (workspace) | 167→| `cru session create` | | Create a new session (add `--agent ` for ACP) | 168→| `cru session list` | `s` | List sessions (live by default, `--all` includes persisted) | 169→| `cru session show ` | | Show session details (daemon first, file fallback) | 170→| `cru session open ` | | Open a previous session in the TUI | 171→| `cru session send "msg"` | | Send a message and stream the response | 172→| `cru session configure ` | | Set agent backend (provider, model, endpoint) | 173→| `cru session pause ` | | Pause a running daemon session | 174→| `cru session resume ` | | Resume a paused daemon session | 175→| `cru session end ` | | End a daemon session | 176→| `cru session export ` | | Export session to markdown | 177→| `cru session search ` | | Search sessions by title | 178→| `cru set key=val` | | Tweak runtime settings (model, temperature, etc.) | 179→| `cru stats` | | Display kiln statistics | 180→| `cru status` | | Storage status and metrics | 181→| `cru models` | | List available LLM models | 182→| `cru config init` | `cfg` | Initialize config file | 183→| `cru config show` | | Show effective configuration | 184→| `cru agents list` | | List registered agent cards | 185→| `cru skills list` | | List discovered agent skills | 186→| `cru tasks list` | | Manage tasks from TASKS.md | 187→| `cru daemon start` | | Start background daemon | 188→| `cru daemon status` | | Check daemon status | 189→| `cru storage verify` | | Verify content integrity | 190→| `cru auth login` | | Store LLM provider API key | 191→ 192→Run `cru --help` for full options. 193→ 194→## Roadmap 195→ 196→- [x] TUI chat with session persistence and resume 197→- [x] MCP server for external agents 198→- [x] Lua/Fennel plugin system (17+ API modules) 199→- [x] Block-level semantic search with reranking 200→- [x] Precognition (auto-RAG before each turn) 201→- [x] Daemon with auto-spawn, file watching, multi-session support 202→- [ ] Web chat interface 203→- [x] ACP host mode (use Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode through Crucible) 204→- [ ] ACP agent mode (embed Crucible in editors like Zed, JetBrains, Neovim) 205→ 206→## License 207→ 208→MIT or Apache-2.0, at your option. 209→

data.content: ible is a local-first, knowledge-grounded agent
runtime where your conversations, notes, and wiki
links form a markdown-based knowledge graph that AI agents
draw from and contribute to — no cloud required.
